Impact Rationale: Serious service degradation; 36% of orgs in deficit; beneficiaries lose access.
Size Rationale: Universal: every nonprofit affected; millions of beneficiaries downstream.
Urgency Rationale: Accelerating post-2024 funding cuts (NL dev budget -2/3 over 3 years); deficits widening now.
Feasibility Rationale: Systemic funding cannot be fixed by software; adjacent tools (efficiency, reporting) feasible but don't move the core.
Extent Rationale: Technology can only trim overhead; underlying revenue gap is structural.

Impact Rationale: Real but not life-or-death; systemic underfunding of grassroots is the indirect effect.
Size Rationale: Thousands of orgs in NL, millions globally writing grants annually.
Urgency Rationale: Chronic, not acute; no escalation trigger.
Feasibility Rationale: LLMs tailor-made for this: proposal drafting, funder matching, compliance checks — production-quality in 24h.
Extent Rationale: 60–80% of writing time can be automated; not the same as winning more grants, but directly reduces burden.

Problem: Donor retention decline & shifting giving behaviour
Impact Rationale: Financial health hit; flexible unrestricted revenue disappears first.
Size Rationale: Broad: all fundraising nonprofits across EU.
Urgency Rationale: Declining actively; platform policy changes compound the effect.
Feasibility Rationale: CRM/engagement prototypes feasible; requires donor-data integration to be meaningful.
Extent Rationale: Many platforms exist; marginal differentiation available.

Problem: Digital maturity gap (88% of nonprofits stuck)
Impact Rationale: Efficiency and reach erosion; compounds over time.
Size Rationale: 88% of nonprofits globally.
Feasibility Rationale: Too meta for a 24h hackathon; specific tools help but the gap is organisational.
Extent Rationale: Partial — technology adoption requires culture change.

Impact Rationale: Service capacity constrained; events cancelled.
Size Rationale: 46.8% of nonprofit leaders cite this as top challenge.
Urgency Rationale: Post-COVID decline in formal volunteering; trend continues.
Feasibility Rationale: Matching platforms highly tractable; dozens of competing products.
Extent Rationale: Improves matching quality but structural volunteer decline needs more.

Impact Rationale: Knowledge loss, onboarding tax; less acute than recruitment.
Size Rationale: Most orgs that recruit volunteers; high turnover sector-wide.
Feasibility Rationale: Lifecycle tooling (scheduling, recognition, comms) is well-scoped.
Extent Rationale: Measurable improvement possible; not transformative.

Impact Rationale: Funding-cycle quality; learning loop broken.
Size Rationale: Universal expectation from funders.
Feasibility Rationale: Outcome-tracking demos feasible; integration with funder reporting formats harder.
Extent Rationale: Real efficiency gains if adoption sticks.

Impact Rationale: Mental health, attrition, cascading service degradation.
Size Rationale: Broad across sector; 11%+ vacancy rates common.
Feasibility Rationale: Burnout is not primarily a tech problem; wellness apps exist but impact questionable.
Extent Rationale: Marginal — requires compensation and structural change.

Impact Rationale: Strategic drift, funder-relationship fragility.
Size Rationale: 75%+ of nonprofits report this.
Feasibility Rationale: Hiring is not a hackathon-scale problem for leadership roles.
Extent Rationale: Limited — matching platforms exist.

Impact Rationale: Beneficiary harm (refugees, activists); ICRC-scale breaches documented.
Size Rationale: Only 1 in 10 NGOs trains regularly; attack rate rising.
Feasibility Rationale: Training tools feasible; real security posture requires deeper integration.
Extent Rationale: Partial mitigation possible; elimination impossible.

Impact Rationale: €90–101M/yr compliance cost; narrower impact.
Size Rationale: Medium-sized nonprofits with EU ambitions.
Urgency Rationale: Chronic; no EU statute incoming imminently.
Feasibility Rationale: Compliance-checker demos feasible but legal nuance limits utility.
Extent Rationale: Partial — the policy is the bottleneck.

Problem: Platform ad policy restrictions on nonprofits
Impact Rationale: Donor-acquisition efficiency loss.
Size Rationale: Most digital-fundraising nonprofits.
Feasibility Rationale: Workarounds feasible; structural fix requires platform policy change.
Extent Rationale: Limited — platforms control the rules.

Problem: Food banks reach only 15–17% of eligible population
Impact Rationale: People go hungry who qualify for help; dignity and health harm.
Size Rationale: 100k+ eligible-but-unreached in NL; similar gaps in Belgium/Germany.
Urgency Rationale: Rising cost-of-living expanding the gap now.
Feasibility Rationale: Outreach + eligibility assessment + referral app highly tractable in 24h.
Extent Rationale: Material reach improvement possible — 2–3× current coverage within reason.

Problem: Food bank supply decline as retailers reduce waste
Impact Rationale: Lower nutritional quality for recipients; mission tension (waste vs. aid).
Size Rationale: All Dutch food banks; similar pattern EU-wide.
Urgency Rationale: Structural shift happening now (retail ESG pressure).
Feasibility Rationale: Surplus-food matching apps well-understood; last-mile logistics harder.
Extent Rationale: Real gains possible; cannot create supply that doesn't exist.

Problem: Refugees cannot navigate digitalised Dutch bureaucracy
Impact Rationale: Integration failure: housing, healthcare, education, employment access.
Size Rationale: 150k+ refugees in NL plus annual inflow; scales EU-wide.
Urgency Rationale: Dutch bureaucracy continues digitalising; gap growing.
Feasibility Rationale: AI multilingual procedural assistant for DigiD/Gemeente is ideally suited to hackathon scope.
Extent Rationale: Large — can substantially reduce friction for first-year newcomers.

Impact Rationale: Poverty (25% at/below poverty line), wasted skills.
Size Rationale: 150k+ status holders in NL; 10% employment for recent arrivals.
Feasibility Rationale: Job-matching + skill-recognition demo feasible; diploma recognition is structural.
Extent Rationale: Partial — hiring bias and credentialing are not primarily tech problems.

Problem: Homeless EU citizens denied shelter in Dutch cities
Impact Rationale: Cold-exposure deaths; rough sleeping; 75–80% of rough sleepers in big Dutch cities are EU citizens.
Size Rationale: ~6,000 in NL (rising from 2,500).
Urgency Rationale: Acute: annual winter cycle; policy confrontation now.
Feasibility Rationale: Rights-awareness / navigation app possible but doesn't fix policy.
Extent Rationale: Limited — core issue is municipal exclusion policy.

Problem: Shelter safety deters the most vulnerable from using it
Impact Rationale: Preventable hypothermia, substance-related harm.
Size Rationale: ~40k homeless in NL; subset refusing shelter.
Feasibility Rationale: Shelter design is a physical/organisational problem; tech support role is small.
Extent Rationale: Very limited — requires shelter-model change.

Impact Rationale: Morbidity/mortality link; healthcare utilisation increased.
Size Rationale: Millions of EU seniors; 1 in 11 severely lonely in NL.
Feasibility Rationale: Connection/companion platforms feasible; sensitive design needed.
Extent Rationale: Partial — can complement but not replace human contact.

Problem: Domestic-violence victims not reaching support services
Impact Rationale: Severe injury, femicide, child exposure.
Size Rationale: 100k+ victims in NL; ~3% reach Veilig Thuis.
Urgency Rationale: Acute; escalation daily; femicide cases are current news.
Feasibility Rationale: Anonymous / stealth help app with routing and risk assessment is highly feasible.
Extent Rationale: Material increase in help-seeking possible, but criminal-justice pipeline remains.

Impact Rationale: Deaths from delay in cardiac, stroke, trauma.
Size Rationale: 9.3% of Dutch urgent responses exceed 15 min; millions affected by policy.
Feasibility Rationale: Optimisation demos feasible; data access via RIVM is non-trivial.
Extent Rationale: Partial — cannot fix staffing but can improve deployment.

Impact Rationale: Two-tier emergency care; worse outcomes in rural areas.
Size Rationale: 63 villages + rural population; bounded geographic scope.
Feasibility Rationale: Volunteer-responder networks are the proven intervention but physical.
Extent Rationale: Partial — augmentation, not replacement.

Impact Rationale: Delay in life-threatening calls; reduced bystander CPR for LEP callers.
Size Rationale: Millions of non-Dutch speakers in NL; 28% of abroad-callers face issues.
Urgency Rationale: Rising migrant/tourist population; no systemic fix deployed.
Feasibility Rationale: Real-time AI translation at the call-centre is a flagship hackathon fit; APIs mature.
Extent Rationale: Major — studies show minute-level latency wins save thousands of lives.

Problem: Dispatcher recognition of out-of-hospital cardiac arrest
Impact Rationale: Direct survival link; every minute of delay -7 to -10% survival.
Size Rationale: ~12k OHCAs/year in NL; larger subset where dispatch fails.
Feasibility Rationale: Call-analysis ML demo feasible; integration with CAD harder.
Extent Rationale: Large — decision support at dispatch is well-validated.

Impact Rationale: Time-to-compression is direct survival predictor.
Size Rationale: Majority of OHCA calls.
Feasibility Rationale: Calming-script + instruction optimisation apps feasible; evaluation harder.
Extent Rationale: Meaningful — field trials show seconds-to-minutes gains.

Impact Rationale: Direct survival delay when recognition fails.
Size Rationale: ~30% of OHCAs show seizure-like activity; narrower subset.
Feasibility Rationale: Specific ML classifier on call audio is a well-scoped prototype.
Extent Rationale: Large — targeted intervention on a known failure mode.

Impact Rationale: Suicide, attrition, cascading service impact.
Size Rationale: ~30% of first responders; EU-wide.
Feasibility Rationale: Support apps exist; evidence of effectiveness mixed.
Extent Rationale: Partial — requires organisational + clinical change.

Impact Rationale: Patient-safety (fatigue → errors); workforce shortage feedback loop.
Size Rationale: All ambulance and fire services.
Feasibility Rationale: Fatigue-monitoring, scheduling tools feasible but limited agency adoption.
Extent Rationale: Partial.

Impact Rationale: Resource depletion → downstream delays for real emergencies.
Size Rationale: 74.5% overtriage in documented systems; enormous daily volume.
Urgency Rationale: Worsening in systems with tighter undertriage tolerance.
Feasibility Rationale: Decision-support overlay on MPDS/ProQA is a flagship use case for LLMs.
Extent Rationale: Major — accuracy gains of 10–20% translate to real capacity.

Impact Rationale: Direct delay of life-saving care (STEMI, stroke, anaphylaxis, sepsis).
Size Rationale: 5–28% undertriage rates; thousands of cases per year.
Urgency Rationale: Balance trade-off with overtriage makes this an active problem.
Feasibility Rationale: Same tooling as ES-09; same feasibility.
Extent Rationale: Major — targeted safety uplift.

Problem: Mental-health crisis defaulted to police response
Impact Rationale: Deaths in custody; retraumatisation; arrests instead of care.
Size Rationale: Up to 19% of call volume is behavioural-health related.
Urgency Rationale: Active — US/EU reforms underway; NL lagging on specialised response.
Feasibility Rationale: Dispatch-routing decision support feasible; requires mobile-team deployment to pair.
Extent Rationale: Partial — tech can route but cannot create clinicians.

Impact Rationale: Measurable survival gap at night (worst OHCA outcomes).
Size Rationale: ~1,500 registered AEDs in NL; large fraction off-hours locked.
Urgency Rationale: Every nighttime OHCA; no sector-wide fix deployed.
Feasibility Rationale: Registry + hours + routing is a straightforward hackathon build, can plug into HartslagNu.
Extent Rationale: Major — Toronto study shows 25% coverage uplift possible.

Impact Rationale: Majority of OHCAs are at home; survival much lower.
Size Rationale: Majority of ~12k OHCAs/year in NL occur at home.
Feasibility Rationale: Neighbour AED-sharing + volunteer-responder enhancements are feasible.
Extent Rationale: Partial — cannot place AEDs everywhere; routing gains meaningful.

Problem: Multi-agency information silos in disaster response
Impact Rationale: Delayed situational awareness; duplicated deployments.
Size Rationale: All civil-protection actors in a major incident.
Feasibility Rationale: Interoperability is inherently multi-party; demos feasible but real deployment slow.
Extent Rationale: Partial.

Impact Rationale: Delayed mutual aid; inconsistent civilian messaging.
Size Rationale: Border regions across EU.
Feasibility Rationale: Interoperability at state level is not a hackathon-achievable fix.
Extent Rationale: Limited.

Problem: Elderly/disabled residents under-served in flood evacuation
Impact Rationale: Disproportionate casualty distribution in floods/fires.
Size Rationale: Elderly/disabled in flood zones; a meaningful subset of NL.
Feasibility Rationale: Registry + alert + pickup coordination app is well-scoped.
Extent Rationale: Meaningful — targeted workflow improvement.

Impact Rationale: Longer time to first compression; dispatcher secondary trauma.
Size Rationale: ~32% of callers in OHCA calls; similar in trauma.
Feasibility Rationale: Emotional-state classification + calming scripts feasible.
Extent Rationale: Partial — instrument-level, not structural.

Problem: Bystander AED use remains very low (~4%)
Impact Rationale: Chain-of-survival breaks even when link 1 succeeds.
Size Rationale: Most public OHCAs have an AED nearby but unused.
Urgency Rationale: Chronic; no structural fix in most countries.
Feasibility Rationale: AR guidance + confidence-building + dispatcher coaching are achievable.
Extent Rationale: Meaningful — HartslagNu-style models can push usage to 50%+.

Problem: Wildfire & water-incident early detection in thin-monitoring areas
Impact Rationale: Escalation before recognition; shrinking evacuation windows.
Size Rationale: Increasing NL/Benelux wildfire risk; wider globally.
Feasibility Rationale: Satellite/IoT + ML classifier demo feasible; integration with safety regions harder.
Extent Rationale: Meaningful.

Problem: Domestic-violence cases reach police only in crisis
Impact Rationale: Femicide risk; repeat call-outs.
Size Rationale: Linked to NP-20; 100k+ victims.
Feasibility Rationale: Risk-assessment + repeat-address analytics for police feasible.
Extent Rationale: Partial — tech cannot replace prevention capacity.
